info.title: Wardnet Tenants API
info.description: Wardnet Tenants service — global identity/naming authority + mesh work-queue

post.description: Delete the current session (server-side) and clear the cookie. Idempotent.

get.description: Federated callback: validate state, exchange the code, then either log in (set the session cookie) or — for a mode=link flow — link the verified identity to the current account. 302s back to the account SPA.

302.description: Logged in / linked; redirect to the SPA

401.description: State mismatch / unverified email / exchange failure

409.description: Provider identity already linked to another account

get.description: Begin federated login: stash CSRF/PKCE in a signed cookie and 302 to the provider. With ?mode=link (called while signed in via the session cookie) the callback links the verified identity to the current account.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: "link" to link to the signed-in account

post.description: Log in with email + password. Sets the session cookie on success.
Reset (or set) the account password from a one-time code; force-logs-out every existing session.
Sign up with email + password (the one-time code proves the email). Sets the session cookie on success.
Silent exchange: read the session cookie and mint a short-TTL USER JWT (aud:[tenants]). The SPA holds the JWT only in memory.

get.description: Check whether a vanity slug is available. An invalid or reserved slug reads as unavailable rather than an error.

post.description: Stripe subscription-lifecycle webhook. Unauthenticated: the Stripe-Signature header is verified as the credential. Idempotent.
Bootstrap a daemon: consume a one-time code and bind its public key to a tenant via a short-lived pending record. No JWT is returned — call POST /v1/token next.

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns 200 OK when the bridge process is running. No authentication required. Used by load balancers and uptime monitors.
The current user's account profile (the SPA's identity bootstrap).
List the caller's connected login methods. Never leaks secret hashes.

delete.description: Unlink a login method. At least one login method must always remain.

409.description: Would remove the last login method

post.description: Set or change the caller's password, proven by a fresh one-time email code for the caller's own account email. Revokes every existing session (evicting other devices) and issues a new session cookie for this browser, so the caller stays signed in.

204.description: Password set; a fresh session cookie is issued

delete.description: Sign out of all sessions (including the current one) and clear the cookie.

post.description: Register a network for the calling daemon's tenant and bind the daemon to it. The new network starts in `provisioning`.

409.description: Slug taken / limit reached / daemon already registered

get.description: List a network's daemons (scoped to the caller's tenant).

delete.description: Remove the calling daemon from its network (self-removal on teardown). The row-level effect is idempotent — removing an already-absent daemon still returns 204, though a retry must be freshly re-signed (a byte-identical replay is rejected). Does not deprovision the network or delete its DNS.

403.description: Token is not scoped to this network

get.description: The purchasable plan catalog (ascending by level), each with its live promotion's discounted price. Public; sourced from Stripe via the projection.

delete.description: Deregister (tombstone) the account: all its networks are marked for deprovisioning, its subscription is canceled, and once the networks are fully reaped the account row is swept. Idempotent.

patch.description: Update a tenant. Currently only subscription_status=canceled, which cascades the tenant's networks to deprovisioning.

post.description: Start a Stripe setup-mode Checkout to add/replace the card (no purchase); returns the URL to redirect to.

400.description: Tenant has no billing account yet

post.description: Change an already-paid subscription's plan. Upgrade is immediate; downgrade is scheduled for the period end. Entitlement lands via webhook.

400.description: No paid subscription, unknown plan, or no-op change

409.description: A displayed promo lapsed; re-confirm at full price

post.description: Start a Stripe Checkout for a plan; returns the URL to redirect to. Auto-applies the live promo unless accept_full_price re-confirms.

409.description: A displayed promo lapsed; re-confirm at full price

get.description: Recent invoices, newest first; empty when the tenant has no provider customer. Read-only and provider-proxied.
The tenant's default payment-method summary, or null. Read-only and provider-proxied; never PAN/CVC (SAQ-A).
Provider (Billing) view of the subscription: current Stripe price + any pending scheduled downgrade. Composed by the SPA with /v1/me.

post.description: Issue an add-daemon one-time code for an existing tenant.

delete.description: Mark a network for deprovisioning (the reaper tears down DNS, then deletes the row).

post.description: Mint an identity JWT for a daemon, authenticated by an Ed25519 proof-of-possession signature over the request (headers X-Wardnet-Timestamp / X-Wardnet-Signature). Tenant-scoped before the daemon registers a network, network-scoped after.

401.description: Bad signature / unknown or expired key

post.description: Issue a one-time, email-proof code bound to a purpose (signup / password_reset / enrollment). Public, per-IP rate-limited. Emailed in production; returned here in dev.

available.description: `true` if the slug is well-formed, not reserved, and unused.

BillingSubscriptionView.description:
The provider (Billing) view of a tenant's subscription for the account page —
the bits that live in Billing (Stripe refs), composed by the SPA alongside the
provider-agnostic [`SubscriptionView`]. Response for
`GET /v1/tenants/{id}/billing/subscription`.

current_price_id.description:
The Stripe Price id the tenant is currently subscribed at, or `None` (no paid
subscription — trial/canceled). Lets the SPA highlight the current plan in the picker.

oneOf.description:
A pending scheduled downgrade (read from the Stripe subscription schedule), or
`None`. Survives reloads so the "downgrades on DATE" banner can re-render.

trialing.description:
Whether the subscription is still in its Stripe trial (a *trial-preserving* Home
sub, ADR-0012). The SPA uses this to confirm before an in-app upgrade that would
end the trial — the account state alone can't tell (a honored trial reads `Active`).

accept_full_price.description:
Re-confirm flag after a [`PromoUnavailableBody`] 409 (see
[`CreateCheckoutSessionRequest::accept_full_price`]). Defaults to `false`.

price_id.description: The Stripe Price id of the **target** plan.

ChangePlanResponse.description: Response body for the change-plan endpoint (`202 Accepted`).

current_price_id.description:
The Stripe Price the tenant is on *after* this change — the target for an
immediate upgrade, the unchanged current price for a scheduled/canceled downgrade.
Lets the SPA reflect the new current plan at once instead of waiting on the async
webhook that back-fills `billing_customers` (kills the post-upgrade UI lag).

effective_at.description:
When the change takes effect — the period end for a scheduled downgrade; `None`
for an immediate upgrade or a canceled downgrade.

CheckoutSessionResponse.description:
Response body for the create-checkout-session endpoint. Also returned by the
card-update (setup-mode Checkout) endpoint.

url.description: The Stripe-hosted Checkout URL to redirect the user to.

CodePurpose.description:
The flow a one-time [verification code](VerificationCodeRequest) is bound to
(PR3). A code issued for one purpose can never be consumed by another, closing
cross-purpose replay. Doubles as the `enrollment_codes.purpose` DB value (the
[`ProvisioningState`] pattern). `enrollment` covers both daemon paths (new-signup
and add-daemon), which the orthogonal `tenant_id` column already distinguishes.

CodeResponse.description: Response body for the add-daemon code endpoint.

code.description:
The one-time code — `None` once emailed (production), `Some` in the dev/no-op
email path.

ConnectedIdentityView.description:
One connected login method for `GET /v1/me/identities`. Deliberately omits the
stored secret (invariant #1): never carries a password hash or provider subject.

connected_at.description: When this method was linked to the account.

label.description: A human-facing label — the provider-verified email.

accept_full_price.description:
Set by the SPA only on the **re-confirm** after a [`PromoUnavailableBody`] 409:
the customer has acknowledged the (now full) price, so the server skips applying
any auto-promo and proceeds at the catalog price. Defaults to `false`.

price_id.description: The Stripe Price id of the plan being purchased.

DaemonView.description: The full **Daemon** resource. Producer: Tenants (account-plane listings).

code.description: The one-time code (raw value, as emailed to the tenant).

public_key.description: Base64-encoded raw Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) the daemon will sign with.

tenant_id.description: The tenant this daemon is now (pending-)bound to.

Entitlement.description:
Per-tenant limits. JSONB-stored so new dimensions need no migration; `serde`
defaults keep old rows readable as dimensions are added.

max_daemons.description: Maximum daemons across all the tenant's networks.

max_networks.description: Maximum networks the tenant may hold.

ErrorBody.description: JSON body returned for every error response.

InvoiceStatus.description: Provider-agnostic invoice lifecycle status (mapped from the provider's own set).

InvoiceView.description: One invoice row for `GET /v1/tenants/{id}/billing/invoices` (newest first in the list).

amount_cents.description: Amount in the currency's minor units (e.g. cents); the SPA formats for display.

currency.description: ISO-4217 currency code, lowercase (e.g. `"usd"`), as the provider reports it.

hosted_url.description: The provider-hosted invoice/receipt URL (the download action); `None` if absent.

MeView.description:
Account profile for the SPA (`GET /v1/me`, auth = `USER`). The full current-user
view: the tenant identity + its current subscription.

oneOf.description: The tenant's current (non-`Canceled`) subscription, or `None` if not entitled.

NetworkView.description:
The full **Network** resource. Producer: Tenants (`POST /v1/networks`, the mesh
`GET /v1/networks` work-queue + `GET /v1/networks/{id}` resource read).
Consumers: the DDNS reconciler and the Tunneller routing policy.

PasswordLoginRequest.description: Request body for `POST /v1/auth/password/login`. On success a session cookie is set.

PasswordResetRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/auth/password/reset`. The `code` is the one-time email
proof; the new password replaces (or sets) the account's password and force-logs-out
every session.

PasswordSignupRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/auth/password/signup`. The `code` is the one-time email
proof (gate 1); on success a session cookie is set.

code.description:
The one-time signup code issued to `email` (via
`POST /v1/verification-codes {purpose: "signup"}`).

password.description: Plaintext password (hashed server-side; never stored or logged).

PaymentMethodView.description:
The tenant's default payment-method summary, read back from the provider for
`GET /v1/tenants/{id}/billing/payment-method`.

These fields are **not** PAN/CVC — `last4`/`brand`/`exp_*` are safe to read and render
(SAQ-A preserved; card *entry* still happens only through hosted Checkout/Portal). The
endpoint returns `null` when the tenant has no provider customer / default card yet.

last4.description: The last four digits of the card number, e.g. `"4242"`.

PendingChangeView.description:
A pending scheduled plan change (today only a downgrade), surfaced by
[`BillingSubscriptionView`].

effective_at.description: When the scheduled change takes effect (the current period end).

name.description: The target plan's name (resolved through the catalog projection).

price_id.description: The Stripe Price id the subscription will move to at [`effective_at`](Self::effective_at).

PlanChangeEffect.description:
What a [change-plan](ChangePlanRequest) did. The actual entitlement change lands
asynchronously via the Stripe webhook — this only reports the effect + when it takes hold.

PlanView.description:
One purchasable [plan](`crate::contract`) row for `GET /v1/plans` (newest catalog,
ascending by [`level`](Self::level)). The whole catalog is sourced from Stripe and
served from the Billing projection; this is its public, tenant-agnostic display shape.

amount_cents.description: The plan's list price in the currency's minor units (e.g. cents) — before any promo.

entitlement.description: The limits this plan grants (from the price's `max_networks`/`max_daemons` metadata).

interval.description: Billing interval as Stripe reports it (`"month"` / `"year"`). Never assumed.

level.description:
The unique integer rank that totally orders the catalog (Stripe price metadata
`level`). Higher = more. Drives the upgrade/downgrade decision.

name.description: Human-facing plan name (the Stripe product name).

price_id.description: The Stripe Price id Checkout / change-plan is created against.

oneOf.description:
A live global promotion for this plan, or `None`. **Display-only** — the discount
is re-derived and applied server-side at Checkout/upgrade, never trusted from here.

PromoUnavailableBody.description:
`409` body returned by checkout / change-plan when an auto-promo that was displayed
has lapsed by the time it is applied (Stripe rejects the coupon). The SPA shows the
real price and re-confirms with `accept_full_price = true`. Carries the structured
price so the SPA need not re-fetch the catalog to render the prompt.

actual_amount_cents.description: The plan's full price in the currency's minor units (no discount).

error.description: Machine tag for the SPA to branch on (`"promo_unavailable"`).

PromoView.description:
The display half of a live [promotion](`PlanView::promo`) on a [`PlanView`]. The
discounted amount is computed server-side from the Stripe coupon; the SPA only renders it.

amount_cents_after.description: The post-discount price in the currency's minor units (what the customer pays now).

ends_at.description: When the promotion's window closes (the Stripe coupon `redeem_by`).

label.description: Human-facing label for the promotion (the Stripe coupon name).

ProvisioningState.description:
Network lifecycle. `deprovisioned` is intentionally absent — the reaper's final
transition deletes the row (freeing the slug).

display_name.description: Human-facing name; defaults to the slug when omitted/empty.

region.description: Region that will own this network's DNS/tunnel.

SetPasswordRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/me/password` — an **authenticated** USER sets or changes
their own password. The `code` is a fresh one-time email proof (issued via
`POST /v1/verification-codes {purpose: "password_change"}`) and must be for the
caller's own account email. On success every existing session is revoked and a new
session cookie is issued for the current browser, so the user stays signed in.

code.description: One-time email-proof code for the caller's account email.

password.description: Plaintext new password (hashed server-side; never stored or logged).

SubscriptionStatus.description:
Subscription lifecycle. A tenant's **current** subscription is its single
non-`Canceled` row; losing it (or its cancel) cascades the tenant's networks to
`deprovisioning`.

SubscriptionView.description:
The full **Subscription** resource — the **license** aggregate that grants a
tenant's [`Entitlement`]. Provider-agnostic: payment-provider reference ids
(Stripe customer/subscription/price) live in the **Billing** aggregate and are
surfaced by Billing's own read endpoints, not here. Producer: Tenants (account
plane + embedded in [`TenantView`]). A tenant's *current* subscription is its
single non-`Canceled` row; `Canceled` rows are history and are never embedded as
current.

current_period_end.description: End of the current paid period (a paid subscription only).

entitlement.description: The limits this subscription's plan grants.

trial_expires_at.description: When the free trial lapses (a `Trialing` subscription only).

public_key.description: Base64-encoded raw Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) — the enrolled/registered key.

subscription_status.description: Only `"canceled"` is accepted (cancels the subscription + cascades networks).

VerificationCodeRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/verification-codes` — the unified, `RESTful` one-time
code resource (PR3). `purpose` binds the issued code so it can only be consumed
by the matching flow.

email.description: The account email a code should be issued for.

purpose.description: What the code may be exchanged for (`signup` / `password_reset` / `enrollment`).

code.description:
The one-time code — `None` once it has been **emailed** (production), `Some`
only in the dev/no-op email path so the flow stays exercisable without a mailbox.
